resolution: R6-P1-001 pattern recurs in TWO families (code-graph handlers: 6 un-hardened peers vs 1 hardened query.ts; hooks runtime: Copilot missing compact-cycle trustState writer+reader that Claude and Gemini have). Session handler family has full parity - no asymmetry there. Memory and save families are whole-family gaps, not sibling asymmetries. A third structural asymmetry found: Gemini shared.ts transitively couples to Claude shared.ts instead of owning its own provenance helper.

direction: Session handler family (session-resume vs session-bootstrap vs session-health) sibling-asymmetry
reason: All three route trustState through trustStateFromStructuralStatus with matching 4-state vocabulary; M8 / T-SHP-01 / T-SBS-01 hardening landed consistently
All memory-*.ts files have zero tokens uniformly; no hardened sibling to form asymmetry pair (whole-family gap, not the R6-P1-001 pattern)
All 12 handlers/save/*.ts files have zero tokens; already tracked as whole-family gap under R6-P2-001 rather than sibling asymmetry

direction: Runtime-agnostic hooks (index.ts, memory-surface.ts, response-hints.ts, mutation-feedback.ts) as asymmetry candidates
reason: These are dispatchers/formatters with no parallel sibling pair; memory-surface.ts emits codeGraphStatus but is the single point of derivation, not an asymmetric pair
